context: Hardening follow-on to rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M2. Premortem dab0766 R8 (T2/E1) flagged the wildcard `("*", "get")` at RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns. M3 binary-smoke (rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1-plan/reports/M3-binary-smoke.json:39-87) quantified the FP rate on synthetic Rust .get(<tainted>) callers. This milestone closes ZERO RED tests but improves PRECISION: it reduces the FP rate on real-world Rust codebases where HashMap::get / Vec::get / Option::get / BTreeMap::get are pervasive.

head_state.branch: main (95 commits ahead of origin/main, no push)
head_state.vuln_migration_v1_red_status: 167/168 GREEN (per planning-brief; one retained RED is unrelated to wildcard narrowing)
head_state.rust_vuln_taint_pipeline_v1_state: M5 LOCAL TAG APPLIED at SHA 282ce0b; CHANGELOG entry merged; 4 Rust positive fixtures CLOSED (rust_command_injection_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive, rust_deserialization_positive)

current_member_patterns.verdict: FALSE POSITIVE on HashMap/Vec/Option/BTreeMap callers; intended for receiver-variable member-call HTTP clients (client.get / agent.get) but receiver-name-blind

current_member_patterns.fp_rate_synthetic_pct: UNMEASURED but expected ≥ wildcard get rate; identical receiver-blind shape
current_member_patterns.verdict: EXPECTED-FP on any .post() method on non-HTTP types; HashMap has no `post` method but Vec/Option also lack it — actual blast radius narrower than `get` but still includes user-defined types with `.post()` methods (e.g., custom event-bus DSLs)
TRUE POSITIVE — added in rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M2; closes rust_ssrf_positive

shapes_supported.matching: (rcv_text, field_text) extracted via extract_member_access_receiver_and_field; pat_rcv == "*" matches ANY receiver text

shapes_supported.shape: call-shape with dotted name (e.g., `request.getParameter(...)` as a call_expression)
shapes_supported.matching: split call_name on last `.`; same wildcard semantics as structural

shapes_supported.shape: raw-substring fallback for scoped/qualified module calls

member_patterns_match_semantics.key_finding: member_patterns_match supports receiver-NAME-text matching (`*` = any text, `"client"` = exact text "client"). It does NOT support receiver-TYPE matching (no tree-sitter type inference walked here). Adding type-aware receiver filtering would require non-trivial cross-cutting work (Option C; out of scope for this hardening milestone).

fixtures.src: let key = std::env::var("KEY").unwrap(); let map: HashMap<String,i32> = HashMap::new(); let _v = map.get(&key);
let idx_str = std::env::var("IDX").unwrap(); let idx: usize = idx_str.parse().unwrap(); let v: Vec<i32> = vec![1,2,3]; let _x = v.get(idx);
let k = std::env::var("K").unwrap(); let m: BTreeMap<String,i32> = BTreeMap::new(); let _v = m.get(&k);

fixtures.verdict: TP — closed via scoped-identifier raw-fallback `("","reqwest::blocking::get")`, NOT via the wildcard
TP via FileOpen path; unrelated to HttpRequest wildcard

summary.interpretation: Of 3 .get() member-access fixtures with tainted args, 100% emit Ssrf FPs. The 1 TP path (reqwest::blocking::get) uses scoped-identifier matching, NOT the wildcard. Therefore the wildcard pattern in its current form provides ZERO TRUE-POSITIVE coverage on the synthetic test set — it is pure noise.

wildcard_load_bearing_analysis.question: Does the wildcard close any GREEN test that would regress if removed?
wildcard_load_bearing_analysis.search_strategy: Inspect existing fixtures + e2e tests in crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs for Rust SSRF coverage that depends on member-access shape (`<receiver>.get(...)`) rather than scoped-identifier shape.

rust_ssrf_e2e_test.name: test_e2e_rust_ssrf_reqwest_get
rust_ssrf_e2e_test.fixture_text: fn handler() { let target = std::env::var("URL").unwrap(); reqwest::get(target); }
rust_ssrf_e2e_test.shape: scoped-identifier (reqwest::get is path::path, not member access)

rust_ssrf_positive_fixture.fixture_text: fn handler() { let u = std::env::var("U").unwrap(); reqwest::blocking::get(&u).unwrap(); }

ssrf_string_literal_fp_fixture.wildcard_match: no env_var source / no taint flow even if wildcard matches some `.get`; both layers emit zero per rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M3 binary-smoke `fp_regression_guards_post_m2`

wildcard_load_bearing_analysis.load_bearing_verdict: The `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` wildcards are NOT load-bearing for any currently-GREEN test. All Rust HTTP-client coverage in the test corpus uses scoped-identifier paths (reqwest::get, reqwest::blocking::get, hyper::Client::new), which match via the raw-fallback substring path NOT the wildcard. Therefore removing or narrowing the wildcards is a NON-REGRESSION change for the test suite.

real_world_idioms_potentially_affected.post_narrowing_match: depends on Option choice — Option A keeps `("client", "get")` so still matched
Option A: NOT MATCHED (residual gap; document as known carry-forward — short variable names not covered by name-allowlist)
Option A: NOT MATCHED (member-access receiver text is `self.client`, not in allowlist; document as known carry-forward — depends on receiver-text matching exactness)

option_A_explicit_receiver_names.description: Replace `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` with an enumerated set of common HTTP-client receiver names: `client`, `agent`, `http`, `request_builder`, `req`. Each gets `(name, get)` and `(name, post)` entries.
option_A_explicit_receiver_names.residual_gap: Programmers using non-allowlist receiver names (e.g., `let c = ...; c.get(...)`) won't trigger the sink. Documented carry-forward. Acceptable because (a) idiomatic Rust HTTP usage is `let client = ...`, (b) scoped-identifier paths still cover the module-call form.

option_B_receiver_excludelist.description: Keep `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` but add a NEW field `excluded_receivers: &[&str]` to the AstSinkPattern struct. Wildcard fires UNLESS receiver text matches the excludelist (HashMap, Vec, Option, BTreeMap, IndexMap, HashSet, BTreeSet, etc.).
option_B_receiver_excludelist.loc_delta: +1 struct field, ~30 LOC excludelist + matching logic in member_patterns_match, +12 to +20 excludelist entries
option_B_receiver_excludelist.fp_elimination_pct_on_synthetic: 100 IF excludelist is exhaustive, but receiver text is the variable NAME not the type name — `let m: HashMap<...> = ...; m.get(&k)` has receiver text `m`, NOT `HashMap`. Excludelist on type names doesn't match variable names.
option_B_receiver_excludelist.residual_gap: Excludelist on receiver-VARIABLE-NAMES would need entries like `m`, `map`, `v`, `vec`, `cache`, `dict`, `lookup`, `idx`, `opt` — open-ended; doesn't generalize. Excludelist on receiver-TYPE-text only works on direct constructor invocations like `HashMap::new().get(...)` (uncommon).
option_B_receiver_excludelist.verdict: REJECTED — receiver-NAME-vs-TYPE distinction makes excludelist ineffective without type-walk

option_C_type_aware_receiver_filter.description: Walk up tree-sitter to infer the receiver's type via local variable type annotations or call-site inference; only fire the wildcard if the inferred type is reqwest::Client / ureq::Agent / hyper::Client / etc.
option_C_type_aware_receiver_filter.loc_delta: +200 to +500 LOC type-inference walk + variable-type bookkeeping; new helper functions
option_C_type_aware_receiver_filter.fp_elimination_pct_on_synthetic: Approaching 100, but introduces cross-cutting taint analysis dependencies; affects other languages' wildcard semantics if generalized
option_C_type_aware_receiver_filter.residual_gap: Type inference in tree-sitter alone is unreliable for Rust (turbofish, type erasure, generic params). Would require partial-borrow on rustc-style type info from elsewhere — not available in this crate.
option_C_type_aware_receiver_filter.verdict: OUT OF SCOPE — multi-milestone effort; explicitly excluded from this hardening pass

option_D_remove_wildcards_entirely.description: Remove `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` entirely; rely solely on scoped-identifier paths for HTTP-client detection.
option_D_remove_wildcards_entirely.residual_gap: ANY receiver-variable-bound member-call form (client.get, agent.get, c.get, self.client.get) is no longer matched. Larger residual gap than Option A.
option_D_remove_wildcards_entirely.verdict: REJECTED — too aggressive; loses the load-bearing-on-real-codebases-but-not-tests case of `let client = reqwest::Client::new(); client.get(&url)`

selected_design: Option A — explicit receiver-NAME allowlist for HTTP-client conventions
selected_design_rationale: (1) Eliminates 100% of synthetic FPs (all 3 collection-type member-access fixtures). (2) Preserves the load-bearing real-world idioms `client.get` / `agent.get` / `http.get` where developers use canonical reqwest/ureq/hyper variable names. (3) Smallest LOC delta of all closing options. (4) Additive AND narrowing — preserves all currently-GREEN tests (scoped-identifier paths unchanged), narrows the wildcard surface to known HTTP-client conventions. (5) No struct shape changes, no enum-variant additions, no public-API changes. (6) Residual gaps (`c.get`, `self.client.get`) are acceptable carry-forwards documented in M3-report; future work can layer Option C type-aware filtering on top without conflict.
non_load_bearing_proof: Verified by reading: (a) test_e2e_rust_ssrf_reqwest_get at vuln.rs:1184-1193 uses `reqwest::get(target)` (scoped identifier — does NOT depend on wildcard); (b) ssrf_positive.rs uses `reqwest::blocking::get(&u)` (scoped identifier); (c) rust_ssrf_string_literal_fp lacks env_var source so wildcard match is irrelevant; (d) no other Rust SSRF e2e test exists in vuln.rs. Therefore narrowing wildcards has ZERO impact on currently-GREEN tests.

fixture_wildcard_get_hashmap_post_m2.narrowing_path: receiver text `map` not in allowlist {client, agent, http, request_builder, req}

fixture_wildcard_reqwest_blocking_get_po.narrowing_path: scoped-identifier raw-fallback unchanged; SSRF stays detected

let url = std::env::var("URL").unwrap(); let agent = ureq::agent(); let _r = agent.get(&url);

new_synthetic_fixtures_to_add_for_M2_ver.ssrf_findings_post_narrowing_expected: 0 (documented residual gap; receiver `c` NOT in allowlist)

design_constraints_per_planning_brief.no_new_enum_variants: GUARANTEED — Option A only edits the static AstSinkPattern array
design_constraints_per_planning_brief.additive_OR_narrowing_only_no_loosening: GUARANTEED — wildcards are NARROWED to specific receiver names; no new sinks added that weren't already covered by the wildcard's union
design_constraints_per_planning_brief.no_regression_on_4_rust_red_tests: GUARANTEED — all 4 use scoped-identifier paths, not wildcards
design_constraints_per_planning_brief.no_push_no_publish: USER STANDING RULE; M3 applies LOCAL TAG ONLY
